name: Guidelines on Cybersecurity for the Financial Sector
description: Effective October 4, 2024 (provisional translation). Three sections: Fundamental Principles, Cybersecurity Management Posture (NIST-CSF-like: governance/identify/protect/detect/respond/recover/third-party risk), and FSA-industry cooperation. Widely reported (unverified against this primary text) to contain ~176 discrete response items.
relationToCloudSecurity: See registry/control/fsa.go.jp.json in the SecID repo (CloudSecurityAlliance/SecID) for cloud/AI security relevance notes on this source.
relationToAISecurity: See the paired SecID registry entry.

license.notes: Issued directly by a national government ministry/agency as an official notification/circular -- plausibly excluded from copyright under Japan's Copyright Act Article 13(2) ("notifications, instructions, circulars, and other documents similar thereto issued by national or local government organs"), but this is a less clear-cut case than primary legislation (Art. 13(1)) and has not been legally verified. Treat as moderate-confidence permitted, not certain. Official FSA-published translation, not third-party -- toward the higher-confidence end of this bucket.

current_extraction.notes: Full English text acquired cleanly -- this PDF was NOT encrypted, unlike the cyber.go.jp documents; a plain pdftotext -layout worked on the first try (an earlier WebFetch-based pass in this session had failed to extract its text).

scope.notes: Full text acquired; a good candidate for a future structured per-item extraction (numbered response items).
